heading: Add the Database Access LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) Groups to Atlas
body:
> **Note:** Skip this section if you don't want to
> enable LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization.

> **Important:** 
>
> - You must enable authentication with LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) before enabling
>   authorization.
>
> - When you enable and configure LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization, database users
>   who are only configured for LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authentication will no longer
>   be able to access databases.

Atlas LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) authorization uses LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) groups to determine if
users are authorized to perform database actions.

Create separate Microsoft Entra ID groups for
each level of access that you want to grant to users. For example,
you create one group for read access to one database, another for read
and write access, and so on. Assign users to groups based on the level
of access each user requires.

To create Microsoft Entra ID database access
groups and assign users, see the [Azure documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-groups-create-azure-portal).
